channel.title: Quality3Ds Daily Pulse
channel.description: Curated daily news for 3D printing enthusiasts.

item.title: 3D PRINTING PULSE #64 - 2026-03-12
item.description:
🦺 Leitat advances personalized medical device capabilities through DiMεD network Leitat Technological Center strengthened its capabilities in the development of personalized invasive medical devices through the Cervera DiMεD Network of Excellence, a project financed by Spain’s CDTI (Centro para... Reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 11, 2026. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/DiMεD_Leitat_1772788192626-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/leitat-advances-personalized-medical-device-capabilities-through-dim%ce%b5d-network

🚀 Addiguru enters & expands several partnerships to advance adoption of in-situ monitoring technology Renishaw, Apex Additive, MTC, LISI Aerospace & the University of Bolton are all working with Addiguru. Reported by TCT Magazine on Mar 11, 2026. Image ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/content/images/2026/03/Authentise-and-Addiguru.png Read more ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/addiguru-enters-expands-several-partnerships-to-advance-adoption-of-in-situ-monitoring-technology

🏠 ACMI installs AMCM M 8K metal 3D printing system American Center for Manufacturing and Innovation (ACMI), a U.S. organization focused on strengthening domestic manufacturing through investments in emerging industrial companies and infrastructure, has acquired an AMCM M 8K metal 3D printing system, reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 11, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/AMCM_M8K-150x100.jpg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/acmi-installs-amcm-m-8k-metal-3d-printing-system-249752

🚀 Texas A&M and U.S. Army Develop Foam That Absorbs Ten Times More Energy Researchers at Texas A&M University and the DEVCOM Army Research Laboratory have engineered a composite that absorbs up to ten times more energy than conventional padding, threading a 3D printed elastomeric skeleton... The implications stretch well beyond […], reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 11, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/20251103_Mohammad_Naraghi_Lab_AT_0016-1408x792-1-150x100.jpg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/texas-am-and-u-s-army-develop-foam-that-absorbs-ten-times-more-energy-249780

📰 EPFL develops 3D printable bone scaffold using enzyme-driven mineralization Researchers at the École Polytechnique Fédérale de Lausanne (EPFL) have developed a method to 3D print porous scaffolds made from hydroxyapatite (HA), which is the primary mineral component of bone. … The post EPFL develops 3D printable bone scaffold using enzyme-driven mineralization appeared first on VoxelMatters - The heart of additive manufacturing. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Cylindrical-demonstrations-of-the-3D-printed-scaffolds-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/epfl-develops-3d-printable-bone-scaffold-using-enzyme-driven-mineralization

🖨 New IP Case Against Bambu Lab Heads to Shanghai Court In its latest legal challenge, 3D printer manufacturer Bambu Lab has been sued by Chinese toymaker Pop Mart over copyright infringement claims tied to knockoff Labubu files that were uploaded to Bambu Lab’s... The suit was filed at the People’s Court of Pudong New Area in Shanghai, with the trial scheduled for […], reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 11, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/bb8bbb3f-0c66-4e8f-ac6a-d3ac4d7683cd_edd804f5-150x100.jpg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/new-ip-case-against-bambu-lab-heads-to-shanghai-court-249801

🦺 GELITA and Black Drop collaborate on GelMA bioinks for 3D bioprinting research GELITA, a German manufacturer of collagen proteins and gelatin used in food, pharmaceutical, and biomedical applications, has signed a research and development agreement with Black Drop, a biotechnology company... The collaboration will focus on developing bioinks for biomedical uses, including three-dimensional tissue models and implants, reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 11, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/GELITA_BlackDrop_1_med-150x100.png Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/gelita-and-black-drop-collaborate-on-gelma-bioinks-for-3d-bioprinting-research-249746

🚀 America Makes launches two DoD additive manufacturing project calls worth over $35M America Makes, the U.S. national additive manufacturing innovation institute, and the National Center for Defense Manufacturing and Machining (NCDMM) have announced two new project calls totaling more than $35 million in funding, reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 11, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/America-Makes-facility-face-150x100.jpg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/america-makes-launches-two-dod-additive-manufacturing-project-calls-worth-over-35m-249741

item.title: 3D PRINTING PULSE #63 - 2026-03-11
item.description:
🦺 WSU researchers develop beating, 3D printed heart model for surgical training Researchers at Washington State University (WSU) have developed a 3D printed model of the left side of the heart that contracts and beats, giving surgeons and medical students a reusable, … The post WSU researchers... Reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 10, 2026. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/3d-printing-of-heart-model-e1773137957718-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wsu-researchers-develop-beating-3d-printed-heart-model-for-surgical-training

🚀 Lithoz launching three materials at ceramitec 2026 Ceramic AM pioneer Lithoz is preparing for a big showcase at the upcoming ceramitec 2026 expo. Not only will the Austrian company be bringing a number of demonstrator components—including 46 … The post Lithoz launching three materials at ceramitec 2026 appeared first on VoxelMatters - The heart of additive... Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/LITHOZ_Zirconia_Chainmail-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/lithoz-launching-upgraded-materials-ceramitec-2026

♻️ LAMA brings LFAM of recycled plastics to Latin America with robotic extrusion system LAMA, a Santiago-based company founded in August 2025, is using industrial robotic arms fitted with specialized polymer extruders from the Italian company REV3RD to produce large-format design objects and urban … The... Reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 10, 2026. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/LAMA_3D_Chile_LFAM_IMG_5839-2-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/lama-brings-large-scale-recycled-plastic-3d-printing-to-latin-america-with-robotic-extrusion-platform

📰 rpm and amsight develop 3D printed padding system for EOD helmets Additive manufacturing is being extensively deployed in military applications, across a range of equipment, vehicle parts, UAVs, and much more. Safety gear is one such area, and rapid product manufacturing … The post rpm and amsight develop 3D printed padding system for EOD helmets appeared first on VoxelMatters - The heart of additive manufacturing. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Safety-critical-helmet-padding-using-3D-printing-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/rpm-and-amsight-develop-3d-printed-padding-system-for-eod-helmets

🚀 Nikon AM signs U.S. Defense Innovation Unit contract to reduce production bottlenecks with 3D printing Department of War (DoW) Defense Innovation Unit (DIU) is aiming to tackle production bottlenecks for its aeronautical systems using Nikon Advanced Manufacturing's metal additive manufacturing (AM) technology. The company's Nikon AM Synergy arm, which provides engineering and manufacturing services from its technology centre, reported by TCT Magazine on Mar 10, 2026. Image ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/content/images/2026/03/Nikon-AM-Synergy-Long-Beach-front.JPEG Read more ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/nikon-am-signs-u-s-defense-innovation-unit-contract-to-reduce-production-bottlenecks-with-3d-printing

🖨 [INTERVIEW] Creality CEO pitches long-term growth story as it prepares for IPO Desktop 3D printing has grown quickly, but its place in everyday life remains uncertain. As Creality moves towards a public listing, its founder is asking investors to look past product cycles and short-term results to a harder question: whether the market itself can still expand, reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 10, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Creality-at-Formnext-2025.-Photo-by-Michael-Petch-150x100.jpg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/interview-creality-ceo-pitches-long-term-growth-story-as-it-prepares-for-ipo-249770

📰 3D Printed plug-and-play Packaging Enables low-loss Fiber Coupling for Photonic Chips Researchers led by Erik Jung from Heidelberg University, a German research university with programs in photonics and nanotechnology, together with collaborators including Wolfram Pernice from University of Münster, a... Reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 10, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Plug-and-play-concept.-Photo-via-Science-150x100.jpg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/3d-printed-plug-and-play-packaging-enables-low-loss-fiber-coupling-for-photonic-chips-249763

🚀 3D People deploys AMIS Runtime to automate additive manufacturing build preparation UK additive manufacturing service provider 3D People has deployed AMIS Runtime, a programmable build preparation engine, across its London production facility. Announced on March 3, 2026, the deployment integrates automated build preparation into the company’s polymer powder bed fusion production workflows, reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 10, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/FrontImage_AMIS_Runtime-150x100.png Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/3d-people-deploys-amis-runtime-to-automate-additive-manufacturing-build-preparation-249738

item.title: 3D PRINTING PULSE #62 - 2026-03-10
item.description:
🦺 VoxelMatters Medical AM Focus 2026 eBook As one of the biggest adopters of additive manufacturing, the medical sector is always on our radar and our annual eBooks on the segment reflect that. This year, we are … The post VoxelMatters Medical AM Focus 2026 eBook appeared first on VoxelMatters - The heart of additive manufacturing. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Medical-AM-2026-eBook-Feature-Image-150x150.png Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/voxelmatters-medical-am-focus-2026-ebook

🚀 Technical Review: Evaluating 3D Fuel Pro PCTG for Engineering FDM Applications This article presents a technical evaluation by the 3D Printing Industry engineering team of 3D Fuel’s Pro PCTG, a high-performance thermoplastic filament developed for FDM printing. Pro PCTG is a glycol-modified copolyester engineered to deliver improved toughness, dimensional stability, chemical resistance, and lower moisture sensitivity compared to standard PET and PETG, reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 09, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Benchmark-Test-Standard-Print-Speed--150x100.png Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/technical-review-evaluating-3d-fuel-pro-pctg-for-engineering-fdm-applications-249716

♻️ Airtech to showcase new sustainable composite materials and 3D printed tooling at JEC World 2026 Airtech will present an expanded portfolio of sustainable materials and large-format additive manufacturing applications at JEC World 2026, demonstrating how 3D printed tooling and recycled composite resins can... Reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 09, 2026. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Airtec_JEC2026_-2026-03-05-alle-17.08.11-1-scaled-e1773053964156-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/airtech-to-showcase-new-sustainable-composite-materials-and-3d-printed-tooling-at-jec-world-2026

🖨 INTERVIEW: Will Perseus Materials’ Bet on Self-propagating Chemistry be “world’s fastest” Composite Manufacturing Technology? A Stanford-spinout startup is developing a continuous composite manufacturing process it says can produce structural parts larger than the machine making them, using a self-propagating chemical reaction to eliminate... The chemistry originated at the California university, where researchers were working on a chemically recyclable resin for composite […], reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 09, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/The-Perseus-Materials-team-at-JEC.-Photo-via-Perseus-Materials-150x100.png Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/interview-will-perseus-materials-bet-on-self-propagating-chemistry-be-worlds-fastest-composite-manufacturing-technology-249731

📰 Lithoz presents three ceramic 3D printing material updates The company, which specialises in Lithography-based Ceramic Manufacturing, will present the updates at Ceramitec. Reported by TCT Magazine on Mar 09, 2026. Image ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/content/images/2026/03/LITHOZ_Alumina-and-ATZ_Heartpump-1.jpg Read more ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/lithoz-presents-three-ceramic-3d-printing-material-updates

🚀 Kaneko Optical Launches UROKO, a New 3D Printed Eyewear Collection for ISSEY MIYAKE MEN SS26 Kaneko Optical, a Japanese eyewear brand, has used 3D printing to translate the surface language of Japanese ceramics into wearable form, releasing UROKO, a new limited eyewear model for Japanese designer ISSEY... Eight Lenses, One Ceramic Reference UROKO departs […], reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 09, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Screenshot-2026-03-09-at-15.57.39-150x100.png Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/kaneko-optical-launches-uroko-a-new-3d-printed-eyewear-collection-for-issey-miyake-men-ss26-249704

🖨 Stratasys Achieves Major Reliability Milestone with Industrial Partners 3D printer OEM Stratasys has reported a 22% year-over-year increase in system reliability for its F900 industrial printer, the result of targeted manufacturing upgrades rolled out in 2025 alongside its industrial... The improvements tackled production-scale obstacles directly, giving customers a stronger foundation to expand additive manufacturing into end-use parts and real-world […], reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 09, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/STRP050_F900_with_part_transparent_bkg-150x100.jpg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/stratasys-achieves-major-reliability-milestone-with-industrial-partners-249699

🖨 Ter Hoek places faith in XJet for expansion into ceramics Ter Hoek, a precision manufacturing specialist based in Rijssen, Netherlands, has acquired an XJet Carmel 1400C ceramic 3D printing system, as it expands its production capabilities from metal-based processes into …... Reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 09, 2026. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/XJet-1400C-Alumina-system-installed-at-Ter-Hoek-facility-in-Rijssen-The-Netherlands-e1773071474477-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/ter-hoek-places-faith-in-xjet-for-expansion-into-ceramics

item.title: 3D PRINTING PULSE #61 - 2026-03-09
item.description:
🦺 How 3D Printing Innovation in Prosthetics Led from CU Labs to Hanger’s Acquisition of Point Designs Goulding and Preeti Sulibhavi examine Hanger’s 2025 acquisition of Point Designs, tracing its University of Colorado origins, its advanced 3D printed prosthetic finger portfolio, and how improving federal... In a move that underscores the rapid maturation of 3D printing in prosthetic care, Point Designs… Continue reading How 3D Printing Innovation in Prosthetics Led from CU Labs to Hanger’s Acquisition of Point Designs, reported by Fabbaloo on Mar 07, 2026. Read more ? https://www.fabbaloo.com/news/how-3d-printing-innovation-in-prosthetics-led-from-cu-labs-to-hangers-acquisition-of-point-designs

📰 CityUHK develops 3D printed smart materials modeled on sea urchin spine structure A research team at City University of Hong Kong (CityUHK) has found that the naturally occurring porous ceramic structure inside sea urchin spines can generate measurable electrical voltage in response … The post... Reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 08, 2026. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Professor-Lu-Jian-e1772850304818-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/cityuhk-develops-3d-printed-smart-materials-modeled-on-sea-urchin-spine-structure

📰 CFDC uses 3D printing to reconstruct 9-metre Styxosaurus skeleton A small team of four at the Canadian Fossil Discovery Centre (CFDC) in Morden, Manitoba, is making an extensive effort to reconstruct a 9-metre Styxosaurus skeleton using 3D printing technology … The post CFDC uses... Reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 08, 2026. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/CFDC-Lab-Technician-Gerry-Peters-Volunteer-Ted-Nelson-and-CFDC-Executive-Director-Adolfo-Cuetara-e1772927947616-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/cfdc-uses-3d-printing-to-reconstruct-9-metre-styxosaurus-skeleton

💻 OCL Architectural Lighting Uses 3D Printing for Luminaries OCL Architectural Lighting is using material extrusion to make luminaries. The luminaries are intended for commercial lighting and are available in the Printz line, reported by 3DPrint.com on Mar 08, 2026. Read more ? https://3dprint.com/324417/ocl-architectural-lighting-uses-3d-printing-for-luminaries

📰 Velo3D’s Website Hacked! Velo3D’s website has been hacked. The post Velo3D’s Website Hacked, reported by Fabbaloo on Mar 08, 2026. Read more ? https://www.fabbaloo.com/news/velo3ds-website-hacked

item.title: 3D PRINTING PULSE #60 - 2026-03-08
item.description:
🦺 Xi’an Jiaotong University team bioprints aligned skeletal muscle tissue using electric fields A research team from Xi’an Jiaotong University has developed a method for bioprinting skeletal muscle tissue whose cells align in directions that mirror the architecture of real human muscle. The … The post Xi’an Jiaotong University team bioprints aligned skeletal muscle tissue using electric fields appeared first on VoxelMatters - The heart of additive manufacturing. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Printed-muscle-02-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/xian-jiaotong-university-team-bioprints-aligned-skeletal-muscle-tissue-using-electric-fields

🚀 Rosatom 3D prints pump component for RITM-200 ship reactor Specialists from the Afrikantov Experimental Design Bureau of Mechanical Engineering (OKBM Afrikantov), part of Rosatom’s Mechanical Engineering Division, have 3D printed the impeller wheel of the RITM-200 ship... Reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 07, 2026. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Rosatom-additive-manufacturing-nuclear-industry-component-1-150x150.jpeg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/rosatom-3d-prints-pump-component-for-ritm-200-ship-reactor

📰 BMW Group targets 2027 for WAAM series production BMW Group is moving to begin series production of components using wire arc additive manufacturing (WAAM) from 2027, marking a significant milestone in the car manufacturer’s efforts to industrialize 3D … The post... Reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 06, 2026. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/bmw-additive-manufacturing-metal-scaled-1-e1772793062559-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/bmw-group-targets-2027-for-waam-series-production

📰 BMW says "broad industrialization of additive manufacturing" is on track BMW Group Head of Additive Manufacturing also gave an update on WAAM integration. Reported by TCT Magazine on Mar 06, 2026. Image ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/content/images/2026/03/P90631016_highRes_additive-manufacturi.jpg Read more ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/bmw-says-broad-industrialization-of-additive-manufacturing-is-on-track

💻 Norsk Titanium and Airbus strengthen collaboration with DED agreement Norsk Titanium and Airbus have signed a collaboration agreement to jointly develop and document the Direct Energy Deposition (DED) process for Norsk Titanium’s Rapid Plasma Deposition (RPD) technology. As part … The post Norsk Titanium and Airbus strengthen collaboration with DED agreement appeared first on VoxelMatters - The heart of additive manufacturing. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Production-floor-at-Norsk-Titaniums-Plattsburgh-New-York-Demonstration-Qualification-Center-e1772791558379-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/norsk-titanium-and-airbus-strengthen-collaboration-with-ded-agreement

🚀 What RAPID + TCT’s Essential AM Conference Track Is Really About Ask anyone who has attended RAPID + TCT across multiple years what keeps them coming back, and the answers converge on the same thing: the calibre of conversation the event makes possible. Engineers talk to executives, reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 06, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/SME-RapidTCT-2025_0092-150x100.jpg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/what-rapid-tcts-essential-am-conference-track-is-really-about-249641

🖨 Application spotlight: Metal AM yields cycle time reduction for underground sprinkler mould inserts Xact Metal's XM200G system & Uddeholm Corrax steel were used to manufacture the tooling component. Reported by TCT Magazine on Mar 06, 2026. Image ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/content/images/2026/02/Picture7-2-1.jpg Read more ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/application-spotlight-metal-am-yields-cycle-time-reduction-for-underground-sprinkler-mould-inserts

🚀 Nikon participates in Vast’s latest $500 million funding round Vast has secured $500 million in funding to advance production of its Haven space stations, with plans to launch Haven-1 in 2027 and establish a continuous human presence in low-Earth … The post Nikon participates in... Reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 06, 2026. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/1772719389245-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/nikon-participates-in-vasts-latest-500-million-funding-round

item.title: 3D PRINTING PULSE #59 - 2026-03-07
item.description:
🦺 When No Two Spines Are Alike: Inside the First AI-Designed Cervical Implant Goulding and Preeti Sulibhavi unpack how AI-driven design and additive manufacturing are changing how surgeons think about fit, fusion, and recovery. The post When No Two Spines Are Alike: Inside the First AI-Designed Cervical Implant appeared on Fabbaloo. Read more ? https://www.fabbaloo.com/news/when-no-two-spines-are-alike-inside-the-first-ai-designed-cervical-implant

🚀 ULAS to design Ireland’s first 3D printed liquid rocket engine ULAS HiPR, the University of Limerick’s (UL) student-led high-powered rocketry team, is designing and producing what it claims is the first additive manufactured liquid rocket engine in the Republic of … The post... Reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 06, 2026. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/ULAS-HiPR-01-e1772797197153-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/ulas-to-design-irelands-first-3d-printed-liquid-rocket-engine

🏠 Feature Suggestion: Automatic Random Model Placement There’s a feature I want in every FFF 3D print slicing tool, and so should you. The post Feature Suggestion: Automatic Random Model Placement appeared on Fabbaloo. Read more ? https://www.fabbaloo.com/news/feature-suggestion-automatic-random-model-placement

💻 Built to be worn: how WAZP rethought 3D printed footwear Most people never stop to think twice about how their everyday products are made. The chair you are sitting on, the phone in your hand, reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 06, 2026. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Zrzut-ekranu-2026-03-05-203852-150x150.png Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/built-to-be-worn-how-wazp-rethought-3d-printed-footwear

📰 Rule 1 Ventures Backs Roboze to Bring Mission-Critical Production Closer to the Point of Need Rule 1 Ventures, a U.S. venture capital firm specializing in national security technologies, has made a new investment in Roboze, a company developing production platforms for mission-critical industries, reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 06, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/1772114472053-e1772808407532-150x100.jpeg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/rule-1-ventures-backs-roboze-to-bring-mission-critical-production-closer-to-the-point-of-need-249665

💻 Adidas Scales New 3D Printed CLIMACOOL LACED Globally for Post-Game Comfort Two years after a limited release, German sportswear brand adidas is putting the CLIMACOOL LACED into wide circulation, available globally from March 3, 2026 online and in stores. The shoe is built using Digital Light Synthesis (DLS) 3D printing process, allowing each pair to be assembled in approximately 24 hours into a seamless, joint-free structure […], reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 06, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/713981-150x100.jpg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/adidas-scales-new-3d-printed-climacool-laced-globally-for-post-game-comfort-249658

📰 Five designers embracing 3D printing, from Iris van Herpen to Issey Miyake As we kick off our Consumer AM focus month, we want to take a look at a growing phenomenon in the world of 3D printing: that of notable designers embracing … The post Five designers embracing 3D printing, from Iris... Reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 06, 2026. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/issey-miyake-2-UROKO-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/five-designers-embracing-3d-printing-iris-van-herpen-issey-miyake

🚀 How ACMI’s new AMCM M 8K expands domestic aerospace and defense manufacturing capacity In late January, ACMI has acquired the AMCM M 8K, becoming the first organization worldwide to deploy the large-format laser powder bed fusion system. The acquisition falls into ACMI’s strategy … The post How ACMI’s new AMCM M 8K expands domestic aerospace and defense manufacturing capacity appeared first on VoxelMatters - The heart of additive manufacturing. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/AMCM_M8K-copia-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/how-acmis-new-amcm-m-8k-expands-domestic-aerospace-and-defense-manufacturing-capacity

item.title: 3D PRINTING PULSE #58 - 2026-03-06
item.description:
🦺 No room for guesswork: Bambu Lab H2D in service of veterinary orthopedic surgery How 3D printing lets Dr. Sebastian rehearse surgery before it starts, reported by Bambu Lab on Mar 05, 2026. Image ? https://blog.bambulab.com/content/images/2026/03/Dr-Sebastian-01.jpg Read more ? https://blog.bambulab.com/no-room-for-guesswork-bambu-lab-h2d-in-service-of-veterinary-orthopedic-surgery

Building on the flight-proven H11 variant, the H13 […], reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 05, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/UrsaMajorsHadleyH13-150x100.jpg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/ursa-majors-h13-engine-completes-first-hot-fire-tests-249605

🏠 From Six Weeks to Ten Hours: SPEE3D Is Closing the U.S. Army’s Forward Logistics Gap When a critical component fails in a contested environment, standard U.S. Army logistics can take six to ten weeks to deliver a replacement, time a deployed unit cannot afford, reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 05, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/EMU_Side_by_Side_Bush_Logo_HiRes_Cropped-150x100.jpg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/from-six-weeks-to-ten-hours-spee3d-is-closing-the-u-s-armys-forward-logistics-gap-249613

Image ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/content/images/2026/03/DN-Solutions-Europe.jpeg Read more ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/dn-solutions-opens-its-first-european-additive-manufacturing-centre

The printer represents a technological shift that could reshape how small studios and product designers approach … The post iNEW3D QC2A brings photopolymer color jetting to desktop for under $10K appeared first on, reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 05, 2026. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Discover-how-the-iNEW3D-QC2A-printer-makes-full-color-3D-printing-accessible-with-a-groundbreaking-price-and-technology-for-under-10K-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/inew3d-qc2a-brings-photopolymer-color-jetting-to-desktop-for-under-10k

Reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 05, 2026. Reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 05, 2026. 📰 Ceramic AM market shifts toward services as production scales The technical ceramic additive manufacturing market reached $186.5 million in 2025, growing 13.5% year-on-year. More significantly, the market structure has fundamentally shifted: services revenue ($75.8 million, +22.3% YoY) now nearly … The post Ceramic AM market shifts toward services as production scales appeared first on, reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 05, 2026. item.title: 3D PRINTING PULSE #57 - 2026-03-05
item.description:
🦺 ETH Zurich-Led Study Produces Functional 3D Printed Ear Cartilage, Paving Way for Clinical Reconstruction Researchers from ETH Zurich, the Friedrich Miescher Institute, and the Cantonal Hospital of Lucerne have leveraged 3D printing to engineer elastic ear cartilage with mechanical properties closely matching natural tissue. Using human ear cartilage cells, the team produced constructs that retained shape and flexibility in animal models for six weeks, marking a significant step toward […], reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 04, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/image.imageformat.carousel.500290150-150x100.jpg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/eth-zurich-led-study-produces-functional-3d-printed-ear-cartilage-paving-way-for-clinical-reconstruction-249532

🚀 Conflux Technology 3D printed oil cooler completes full-distance endurance race on Multimatic-engineered car Conflux Technology, an Australian engineering company that develops 3D printed heat exchangers, has completed a full-distance endurance race with a configurable transmission oil cooler installed on a Multimatic... Built using its configurable core platform, the transmission cooler was adapted to the programme’s specific boundary conditions and produced in two weeks using metal […], reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 04, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Conflux-oil-cooler-used-in-a-full-distance-endurance-race-on-a-Multimatic-engineered-car.-Photo-via-Conflux-Technology-150x100.png Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/conflux-technology-3d-printed-oil-cooler-completes-full-distance-endurance-race-on-multimatic-engineered-car-249570

The post Third-Party Software Marks New Phase in 3D Concrete Printing Development appeared on Fabbaloo. Read more ? https://www.fabbaloo.com/news/third-party-software-marks-new-phase-in-3d-concrete-printing-development

💻 AMIS Runtime: no more manual build preparation in industrial 3D printing The 3D printing industry has always been obsessed with machines and materials, leaving software quietly in the background. For years, everyone pretended the problem didn’t exist, or that it simply … The post AMIS Runtime: no more manual build preparation in industrial 3D printing appeared first on VoxelMatters - The heart of additive... The shoe … The post adidas drops latest 3D printed CLIMACOOL LACED sneaker appeared first on VoxelMatters - The heart of additive manufacturing. 📰 AM I Navigator and Leading Minds form alliance to support industrial adoption of additive manufacturing Two industry initiatives focused on additive manufacturing adoption have announced the creation of the Additive Manufacturing Alliance, a framework intended to coordinate efforts supporting the industrial adoption of... The alliance brings together Leading Minds, a consortium of additive manufacturing companies focused on industry awareness and communication, and the AM I Navigator, a maturity […], reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 04, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/ams-2026-karsten-heuser-brigitte-de-vet-leading-minds-am-i-navigator-150x100.webp Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/am-i-navigator-and-leading-minds-form-alliance-to-support-industrial-adoption-of-additive-manufacturing-249557

📰 AMUG announces keynote between General Atomics Aeronautical Systems and Divergent The Additive Manufacturing Users Group (AMUG), a nonprofit organization that promotes industrial additive manufacturing education and collaboration, has announced a second keynote presentation for the 2026 AMUG... Steve Fournier of General Atomics Aeronautical Systems and Scott Sawyer of Divergent will deliver the keynote on March 17, describing […], reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 04, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Fournier-Sawyer-keynote-150x100.jpg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/amug-announces-keynote-between-general-atomics-aeronautical-systems-and-divergent-249563

💻 From Download to Deadly: California Sues Operators of 3D Printed Gun Network California officials have filed a civil lawsuit against an Orlando-based corporation and its affiliated companies and individuals. The state accuses them of running an open internet distribution network for digital blueprints used to manufacture 3D printed ghost guns and prohibited firearm accessories, in violation of state law, reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 04, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/2022-hcr-001-150x100.jpg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/from-download-to-deadly-california-sues-operators-of-3d-printed-gun-network-249575

item.title: 3D PRINTING PULSE #56 - 2026-03-04
item.description:

The mission, titled That’s Not A Knife, lifted off at 7pm Eastern on February 27 aboard Rocket Lab’s HASTE vehicle from Rocket Lab Launch […], reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 03, 2026. The post Detoxified Asbestos Cement Becomes PLA Filler For 3D Printing appeared on Fabbaloo. Read more ? https://www.fabbaloo.com/news/detoxified-asbestos-cement-becomes-pla-filler-for-3d-printing

💻 Issey Miyake 3D prints multi-lens ceramics inspired glasses Issey Miyake Eyes has released UROKO, a pair of 3D printed sunglasses that are built around eight concave lenses, as part of its men’s Spring Summer 2026 collection titled Dancing … The post Issey Miyake 3D prints... Reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 03, 2026. 🖨 3D Printed Ghost Guns Are Hard to Trace. Researchers May Have Found a Way In A team of researchers in Australia has taken a closer look at a problem that is becoming more visible in forensic casework: how to analyse 3D printed firearms when the usual methods no longer work. Led by scientists at Curtin University, the study focuses on the plastics used in consumer-grade 3D printers, reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 03, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/1-s2.0-S2468170926000032-gr1-150x100.jpg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/3d-printed-ghost-guns-are-hard-to-trace-researchers-may-have-found-a-way-in-249509

💻 Sözer speeds jewelry production by 70% with 3D printing and digital automation Turkish jewelry brand Sözer has been around for over 40 years and has built a global reputation as a specialist in gold chains and other fine jewelry. The company was … The post Sözer speeds jewelry production by 70% with 3D printing and digital automation appeared first on VoxelMatters - The heart of additive manufacturing. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/soezer-gold-chains-jewerly-model-150x150.jpeg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/sozer-speeds-jewelry-production-70-percent-3d-printing-digital-automation

🖨 California Targets 3D Printers in New Bill as States Diverge on Gun Control California policymakers are targeting the intersection of consumer 3D printing and firearm production with a bill requiring 3D printers sold in the state to screen what they are asked to produce. Assembly Bill 2047, introduced by Assembly Member Bauer-Kahan, sets out a system built around software rather than hardware alone, reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 03, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/01/9359528e2eacf3d0712e3dad0a083da6_preview_featured-150x100.jpg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/california-targets-3d-printers-in-new-bill-as-states-diverge-on-gun-control-249507

💻 3D People deploys AMIS Runtime in London production facility The AM service provider aims to deliver faster turnaround times and higher consistency. Reported by TCT Magazine on Mar 03, 2026. Image ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/content/images/2026/03/IMG_5300.jpeg Read more ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/3d-people-deploys-amis-runtime-in-london-production-facility

item.title: 3D PRINTING PULSE #55 - 2026-03-03
item.description:
🦺 CollPlant targets formulation bottleneck with new DLP bioprinting kit Regenerative medicine specialist CollPlant has launched BioFlex, a ready-to-print bioprinting kit built on rh-collagen, aimed at researchers working on tissue engineering and regenerative medicine. The kit is designed for Digital Light Processing (DLP) bioprinting and targets two applications: tissue model biofabrication for drug discovery, and the development of engineered tissues and transplantable organs, reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 02, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/f6e374c880374b92f6aa0e963d0fd601-150x100.webp Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/collplant-targets-formulation-bottleneck-with-new-dlp-bioprinting-kit-249501

🚀 Laser 3D printing turns simulated Moon dust into heat-resistant structures Researchers at Ohio State University have used a laser 3D printing process to transform simulated lunar soil into durable, heat-resistant structures, as part of a long-term NASA goal to support … The post Laser 3D... Reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 02, 2026. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Lunar-surface-under-starlit-sky-e1772448148247-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/laser-3d-printing-turns-simulated-moon-dust-into-heat-resistant-structures
